verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a p24 family transmembrane adaptor protein resident in the ER and Golgi compartments, where it functions in COPII-mediated anterograde transport. Its single transmembrane topology is directed toward the endomembrane system (ER/Golgi lumen), not the plasma membrane outer leaflet. No documented cell-state-induced surface display, tissue-restricted surface expression, lysosomal exocytosis pathway, PM cycling, or stable surface anchoring. No therapeutic programs target it on the cell surface. Consistent with its role as a constitutive intracellular cargo adaptor.
